task_completion.description: Agent configures a script or tool to crawl the target website and count input fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor or scraping tool showing a script/configuration targeting 'https://books.toscrape.com/' and extracting input elements (e.g., using XPath 'count(//input)').

task_completion.description: Agent executes the crawl across multiple pages of the site.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or tool output showing active fetching/crawling of multiple pages from books.toscrape.com.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the crawl results to the specified CSV file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File explorer or terminal showing 'form_complexity.csv' successfully created in the '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: The CSV file contains the correct mapped data showing 0 inputs for the crawled pages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Contents of 'form_complexity.csv' displayed on screen, showing multiple rows (at least 20) with URLs from the target site and an input count of '0' for every page.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the max inputs report with the correct maximum integer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Contents of 'max_inputs.txt' in '~/Documents/SEO/reports/' displayed on screen, containing exactly the integer '0' and nothing else.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely interacted with or fetched data from the target website.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of a script running, network requests, or scraping tool logs showing actual HTTP requests to books.toscrape.com, rather than just manually typing out a CSV.

integrity.description: Agent derived the max inputs value from the crawl results rather than guessing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent calculates the maximum value from the generated data (e.g., using a script or spreadsheet function) before writing to max_inputs.txt, rather than blindly echoing '0' into the file.

integrity.description: Agent used the exact requested directory paths.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal commands or file explorer windows confirming the exact paths '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' and '~/Documents/SEO/reports/' were used, not aliases or incorrect folders.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target website https://books.toscrape.com/ has exactly 50 pages of product listings. Crucially, there are ZERO <input> HTML elements on any page of the entire site. A correct crawl will return 0 for every page. The resulting CSV should have multiple rows (at least 20) with 0 as the input count. The max_inputs.txt file must contain exactly the integer '0'.
